reforms.description: The federal government should provide funding to states and localities to develop programs aimed at boosting the human capital of unskilled immigrant workers through training subsidies, alongside a request for $100 million in support for charter schools to establish an additional 3,000 charter schools by the end of the decade.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Investing in human capital through training and education enhances the skills of the workforce, leading to increased productivity and innovation.
reforms.status_evidence: The funding and support for charter schools are part of the announced budget for FY 1998.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reforms significantly enhance human capital development but do not represent a structural overhaul.
reforms.source_quote: The federal government should provide the affected states and localities with funding for them to develop programmes to boost the human capital of such workers, for example, through training subsidies.

reforms.description: Legislation is being considered to transform the Patent and Trademark Office into a semi-autonomous public corporation linked to the Commerce Department, which would include a requirement for patents to be published 18 months after filing.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Transforming the Patent Office aims to enhance the efficiency of the patent system, facilitating knowledge transfer and commercialization of innovations.
reforms.status_evidence: Legislation is being considered, indicating a formal process is underway.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant structural change in the patent system, enhancing its role in innovation.
reforms.source_quote: Legislation which has passed both House and Senate Committees would turn the Patent and Trademark Office into a semi-autonomous public corporation linked to the Commerce Department.

reforms.description: The Small Business Administration (SBA) initiated the Angel Capital Electronic Network (ACE-Net), a nationwide Internet listing of small innovative companies aimed at facilitating connections between entrepreneurs and angel investors.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Facilitating connections between entrepreneurs and investors enhances the startup ecosystem, promoting innovation and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The ACE-Net initiative has been launched and is operational.
reforms.importance_rationale: While beneficial, this initiative is a minor adjustment to existing support mechanisms for startups.
reforms.source_quote: In setting up the ACE-Net, State Securities Administrators agreed to remove restrictions on inter-state trading of unregistered stocks of those companies which are on the ACE-Net.

reforms.description: The Advanced Technology Program (ATP) was created to provide funding that assists US businesses in applying or commercializing new scientific discoveries.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Funding for the application and commercialization of scientific discoveries directly supports innovation and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The ATP has been established and is operational.
reforms.importance_rationale: This program represents a significant funding initiative but does not constitute a major overhaul of the funding landscape.
reforms.source_quote: ATP provides funding to assist US businesses to apply or commercialise new scientific discoveries.

reforms.description: State and local authorities support science parks through the provision of infrastructure, tax breaks, and other fiscal incentives to promote technological development and job creation.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Investment in science parks enhances research infrastructure, fostering innovation and economic development.
reforms.status_evidence: Support for science parks is actively being provided by state and local authorities.
reforms.importance_rationale: This initiative significantly supports research infrastructure but does not represent a major systemic change.
reforms.source_quote: State and local authorities support science parks through the provision of infrastructure and land, tax breaks and tax holidays, promotion – primarily through marketing campaigns and lobbying – and other fiscal and physical incentives.
